description:
An open-source alternative to Claude Cowork, powered by OpenCode

OpenWork is an extensible, open-source “Claude Work” style system for knowledge workers.
It’s a native desktop app that runs OpenCode under the hood, but presents it as a clean, guided workflow:
- pick a workspace
- start a run
- watch progress + plan updates
- approve permissions when needed
- reuse what works (templates + skills)
The goal: make “agentic work” feel like a product, not a terminal.
Highlights
- Harden Den desktop handoff across the browser proxy and keep browser/API base URLs distinct so sign-in and worker launch flows stay reliable.
- Restore a cleaner session experience with the broken artifacts rail removed, a flatter reload-required banner, polished run status, and composer focus preserved after command actions.
Fixes & polish
- Fix the Vercel app build and deploy context so web releases build with the right package inputs.
- Send Den and mobile landing CTAs directly to app.openworklabs.com and keep onboarding worker names in sync.
- Simplify the OpenWork Share OG preview card and update the share page test/simulator coverage around preview rendering.
Packaging / ops
- Bump all shipped package versions to 0.11.169 and refresh the desktop lockfile, AUR metadata, and release packaging inputs.
- Add architecture notes for opencode-router routing and keep download stats current.
Compare:
https://github.com/different-ai/openwork/compare/v0.11.168...v0.11.169
